tests.test_body:
define <4 x i32> @select_or_vec(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_or_vec(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[OR:%.*]] = or <4 x i32> [[Y:%.*]], [[X:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <4 x i32> [[OR]]
  %or = or <4 x i32> %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp ne <4 x i32> %or, zeroinitializer
  %ret = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %or, <4 x i32> %y
  ret <4 x i32> %ret
}

define i32 @select_icmp_and_eq(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_icmp_and_eq(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[AND:%.*]] = and i32 [[A:%.*]], [[B:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TOBOOL:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[AND]], -1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[COND:%.*]] = select i1 [[TOBOOL]], i32 [[A]], i32 -1
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[COND]]
  %and = and i32 %a, %b
  %tobool = icmp eq i32 %and, -1
  %cond = select i1 %tobool, i32 %a, i32 -1
  ret i32 %cond
}

define i32 @select_icmp_or_ne(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_icmp_or_ne(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
  %or = or i32 %a, %b
  %tobool = icmp ne i32 %or, 0
  %cond = select i1 %tobool, i32 0, i32 %a
  ret i32 %cond
}

define i32 @select_icmp_or_eq(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_icmp_or_eq(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
  %or = or i32 %a, %b
  %tobool = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %cond = select i1 %tobool, i32 %a, i32 0
  ret i32 %cond
}

define i32 @select_or_not_1(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_or_not_1(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[Y:%.*]]
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %ret = select i1 %cmp, i32 %or, i32 %y
  ret i32 %ret
}

define i32 @select_icmp_or_eq_commuted(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_icmp_or_eq_commuted(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
  %or = or i32 %a, %b
  %tobool = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %cond = select i1 %tobool, i32 %b, i32 0
  ret i32 %cond
}

define i32 @select_or_not_2(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_or_not_2(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[Y:%.*]]
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %or, 0
  %ret = select i1 %cmp, i32 %y, i32 %or
  ret i32 %ret
}

define <2 x i16> @select_icmp_or_eq_vec(<2 x i16> %a, <2 x i16> %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @select_icmp_or_eq_vec(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i16> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <2 x i16> %a, %b
  %tobool = icmp eq <2 x i16> %or, zeroinitializer
  %cond = select <2 x i1> %tobool, <2 x i16> %a, <2 x i16> zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i16> %cond
}

issue.title: [InstCombine] Missing fold for (a | ~b) ? 0 : a
issue.author: k-arrows

comments.author: floatshadow
comments.body:
It seems gcc will optimize it into `xor  %eax,%eax`, but failed to recognize this similar pattern with vector type version.
https://godbolt.org/z/hxbqhad7q

clang also miss the optimization for ` return (a | b) ? 0 : a;`
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/XTmRx6


I'll check this further as in case of continues ORs, former InstCombine iteration may rotate OR `a` operation to the outermost
https://godbolt.org/z/TsjPrM84K

comments.author: floatshadow
comments.body:
If we have mutiple ORs like `(a | b | c | d) ? 0 : a`. It's quite reasonable to expect we can optimize it to 0 as well.
In simple cases, `visitOr` will invoke `reassociateForUses` . The later would reassociate ORs and expose `a` to the outermost as `a` has the mutiple uses ( (A op B) op C --> (B op C) op A). 
But if B has mutiple uses, things is different, see https://godbolt.org/z/eYG9cqvne. 
- First InstCombine still put `a` to the outer most.
- A later ReAssociatePass put `a` back to the inner, and becomes (B op A) op C
- InstCombine put `b` to the outermost (C op A) op B, just as first instcombine

If we change the order into (b | a | c), the thing becomes worse. `a` can not be exposed to the outermost OR, which means we can not do our target optimization in the very first InstCombine iteration.

GCC still find this pattern in both cases and simply ret 0

It is a brute force method if we recursively check whether it is a multiple OR sequence. 
Maybe we utilize `simplifyWithOpReplaced`.
In `(a | b) == 0? a : 0`. We hope simplify `a` to 0 by replace `(a | b) ` by 0. Replace TrueVal in InstSimplify is covered with AllowRefinement = true, but this branch does not handle this 'reduction' case above. 
`simplifyWithOpReplaced` is done by recusively with AllowRefinement = true, but each replacing only looks at one past instruction, thus we can not replace `a` in `a | b` with 0.
Or maybe do special judge in `simplifySelectWithICmpCond`

comments.author: vfdff
comments.body: Don't address the recusive issue according the discussion on D148986
